question.text: Priya, a family office advisor, is evaluating an AIF's fee structure. Which fee arrangement BEST aligns the fund manager's interests with investors?

answer.text: High fixed management fee with no performance fee
No management fee and no performance fee
Performance fee calculated on unrealized gains without any hurdle
Reasonable management fee with performance fee subject to a hurdle rate and clawback provision

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A hurdle rate ensures the manager earns performance fees only after delivering minimum returns, and a clawback provision recovers excess fees if later investments underperform — both create strong alignment. **The Trap:** High fixed fees without performance linkage create no incentive for outperformance. Performance fees on unrealized gains incentivize mark-up rather than actual exits. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series X-B (Investment Adviser - Level 2) covers fee alignment in advisory relationships. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always explain the waterfall structure to investors — European waterfall (whole-fund) provides better protection than American waterfall (deal-by-deal).
